page: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8488C processors running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7i.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance across multiple vectors. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks show strong results in the top 25%, aided by the high-speed DDR5 memory. Compute-heavy workloads, including Passmark CPU tests, encryption, and physics simulations, also rank in the top 25%. The hardware-assisted matrix extensions contribute to strong LLM inference speeds, particularly for text generation with smaller models, which reaches the top 10% tier. However, multi-core stress-ng tests and large-block memory writes remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency by pairing dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources with high-speed DDR5 memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPUs. This makes it a practical option for workloads that rely on network-attached storage. The instance is suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and LLM inference, with text generation for small models reaching the top 10% tier. Conversely, multi-core stress tests and large-block memory writes perform in the average range. This hardware profile is suited for database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors. It delivers strong database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for enterprise workloads.

192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Strong performance in database operations and memory bandwidth
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and LLM inference
